commit.message:
Rebuild connectivity engine with geometric line tracing

- Fix critical seg.pos vs UF local-index mismatch that corrupted all
  adjacency/label lookups
- Two-pass label assignment: tight direct proximity (0.014×size) + UF
  component propagation; prevents label bleed between parallel pipes
- Add collinearity check (200 UOR off-axis) for same-direction endpoint
  snapping so parallel pipes don't false-connect
- Track shared_endpoints in _build_topology; use directly in
  _detect_terminations to correctly handle crossing-gap connections
- Skip isolated (adj=0) long segments in termination detection — they
  are symbol elements, not open pipe ends
- Create nodes for all 65 classified pipe labels regardless of segment
  assignment so no pipes are silently dropped

Results on M-22EJ01.dgn:
  Named pipe nodes: 65/65 ✓
  Valve nodes:      151/151 ✓
  Terminations:     286 (down from 1200+)
  40-ECB-10" pipe neighbors: 5 (down from 27-34)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Cli auth update (#503)

* cli auth update

* update snippet to ignore startup update

* fix: update bootstrap test for connect command and fix SSH error handling

1. bootstrap.test.ts: Add 'connect' to expectedLeafCommands and update
   expected count from 37 to 38 (new connect command was added)

2. auth-ssh.ts: Wrap runInteractiveSession in try/catch to properly handle
   SSH connection errors. Previously, SSH errors would propagate uncaught;
   now they are caught and presented via io.error before exit(1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Devin review comments

1. connect-daytona.ts: Add try-catch around runInteractiveSession to properly
   handle SSH connection errors (matching auth-ssh.ts behavior).

2. snippets.rs: Move check_for_update_on_startup=false outside the relaycast
   MCP guard so it applies to ALL codex invocations. Previously, if a user
   provided custom mcp_servers.relaycast config, the update suppression was
   skipped, which could cause interactive prompts to hang PTY automation.

3. Update codex_opt_out_when_relaycast_config_already_in_args test to expect
   the update suppression args instead of empty args.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement textproc/splitter for embedding preparation (#38)

The splitter is needed to divide text chunks into sizes suitable for
embedding models. It currently exists only as a reference in
textproc/chunker/doc.go but has no implementation. This completes the
text processing pipeline for embedding generation.

Implementation:
- NewProcessor(src, maxBytes) - creates processor with byte limit
- Splits at sentence boundaries ([.!?。] followed by whitespace)
- Falls back to word boundaries (whitespace)
- Falls back to character boundaries (UTF-8 safe)
- Oversized handling: returns chunks up to 2x limit as-is to avoid tiny fragments
- Trims leading/trailing whitespace, skips empty chunks
- Propagates source errors unchanged

Tests:
- Passthrough: chunks under limit unchanged
- Sentence split: large chunks at sentence boundaries
- Word split: at word boundaries when no sentence
- Character split: at character boundaries (UTF-8 safe)
- Oversized content: emit oversized when word/sentence > limit
- Multiple splits: large chunks into multiple pieces
- Close to limit: chunk slightly over splits into pieces
- Empty chunks: skipped
- Error propagation: source errors passed through
- UTF-8 safety: no multi-byte character breaks

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 552: Historical Genre Placement — OPERATIONAL CONTROL CODEX classification (0 new constraints)

Tier 3 synthesis comparing VMS structural profile against 8 medieval
technical document genres across 7 dimensions. Best match: laboratory
notebooks at 2.5/7. Three VMS properties have zero historical precedent:
structural safety architecture, multi-register architecture, formal
operational grammar. Proposed new genre: OPERATIONAL CONTROL CODEX.
The genre gap is between DESCRIPTION and EXECUTION — publication
(Brunschwig 1500) destroyed proprietary notation's economic rationale.

Fix SignalR relay: add /start call required by F1 protocol

The F1 legacy SignalR protocol requires an HTTP /start call after
WebSocket connection but before data flows. Added /start step,
raw message logging for first 5 messages, and better debug output.

Fix archetype species filter bypassed when species ID is falsy

The old guard `&& character.species?.id` skipped the entire species
check if no species was selected (or if species.id was falsy for any
reason), causing all archetypes to show regardless of species
restrictions. Now matches the ascension tab pattern — always apply
the species filter when the archetype has species restrictions.

SSH module: protect existing config and detect existing keys

Move config deployment from Go runner into install.sh to enable
intelligent handling of existing SSH setups. Detects existing SSH keys
(ed25519/rsa/ecdsa) and auto-selects the type, skipping generation.

user to keep or replace. Backs up existing config before writing.

vars so install.sh can pass custom variables (like DOTFILES_SSH_GITHUB_KEY)
to templates. Updates verify.sh to check the GitHub IdentityFile exists.

commit.message:
feat: Phase 1 blockers resolved — Pandera schemas, dependency fixes

requirements.txt — Python 3.13 compatibility fixes:
  - ta-lib: loosened to >=0.4 (0.6.8 bundles C library, no OS install needed)
  - numpy: >=2.0 (Python 3.13 requires 2.x wheels)
  - pandas: >=2.2 (pandas-ta 0.4.71b0 requires >=2.3.2)
  - torch: >=2.3 (2.10.0 installed, supports Python 3.13)
  - scipy: >=1.13 (1.14+ required for Python 3.13 prebuilt wheels)
  - statsmodels, mlflow, optuna, pydantic, sqlalchemy, et al: loosened to >=min
  - pandas-ta: pinned to 0.4.71b0 (library only ships pre-releases on PyPI)

src/processing/validator.py — Pandera schema contracts:
  - RawOHLCVSchema: all 11 Binance kline columns, OHLCV sanity checks,
    unique open_time, high>=low/open/close invariants
  - RawFundingRateSchema: symbol, funding_time, funding_rate bounds [-1%, +1%]
  - ProcessedOHLCVSchema: cleaned typed OHLCV + era column ('context'|'training'),
    strict=True (no unexpected columns), monotonic open_time
  - AlignedSchema: multi-symbol doge_/btc_/dogebtc_ columns + funding_rate
  - FeatureSchema: all 21 mandatory DOGE features enforced, NaN/Inf checks,
    binary flag columns (0|1 only), regime_label enum check
  - validate() helper with loguru logging on pass/fail

Verified: 12 unit tests collected, 12 skipped. All packages import cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix journal entries pagination + invoice improvements

fix: remove deprecated apple-mobile-web-app-capable meta tag

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: snapshot cleanup + claude install (name-prefix filter) (#2273)

* fix: claude snapshot build — remove npm fallback from install command

The native install (curl | bash) succeeds but exits non-zero due to a
PATH warning. The || fallback then tries `npm install` which doesn't
exist on the "minimal" tier → exit 127.

Fix: replace npm fallback with binary existence check (same pattern
as hermes agent). If install exits non-zero but ~/.local/bin/claude
exists, the build succeeds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: snapshot cleanup and lookup — use name prefix instead of tags

DO Packer builder `tags` only apply to the temporary build droplet,
not the resulting snapshot image. Both the workflow cleanup step and
the CLI's findSpawnSnapshot() were querying by `tag_name` which
returned nothing — old snapshots piled up and the CLI couldn't find
existing snapshots.

Fix: filter by snapshot name prefix (`spawn-{agent}-`) instead of
tags, in both the workflow and the CLI. Remove misleading `tags`
from the Packer template. Add test cases for name-prefix filtering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Iteration 2: Code Mode transform, stateless filters, all Runtime API filters (#1)

* Add Code Mode transform, stateless architecture, and all Runtime API filters

- Add FastMCP CodeMode transform: collapses 8 tools into 3 meta-tools
  (search, get_schema, execute) with 82% schema token reduction
- Remove server-side session tools in favor of stateless architecture
  aligned with MCP ecosystem direction (SEP-1442)
- Add cache_store.py: Redis-backed result cache by API search_id
- Expand to 17 filter dimensions with entity resolution (schools, majors,
  degrees, industries, prev_companies, prev_titles, certifications, etc.)
- Add multi-index fan-out: index='all' searches 6 geographic regions in
  parallel with semaphore, partial results, and round-robin merge
- Enrich candidate summaries: linkedin_url, years_of_experience,
  grad_year, source_index
- Add MCP tool annotations (readOnlyHint, idempotentHint, openWorldHint)
  on all tools
- Fix bug: WholePersonResults -> results (correct JSON key from C# API)
- Include Code Mode validation spike demonstrating the architecture

commit.message:
Fix nebula layer flicker: output alpha=1.0 to stabilize compositor blending

Root cause: the compositor does mix(starfield, scene, scene.a). With additive
blending, scene.a was the SUM of all layer alphas — this fluctuated as
low-res sampling shifted with camera movement, causing starfield to pulse
through overlap regions.

Fix: output alpha=1.0 for any surviving fragment. The compositor sees "fully
opaque" so starfield never bleeds through. RGB stays premultiplied for correct
brightness. Bayer dither restored for retro edge dissolve (only affects the
discard decision, not the alpha output).

commit.message:
Add paint-select and rectangle-select for chunk selection

Cmd/Ctrl+drag paints selection across chunks as you move the mouse.
Shift+drag draws a rectangle preview and selects/deselects all chunks
in the area on release. First chunk determines the action (select vs
deselect) for the entire operation.
